name= and path= validation: mutually exclusive, canonical segment tokens,
and full-path uniqueness.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli v0.1.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli => CLIDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- shared/shared.go --
package shared

import "github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"

//fabrik:cli:command name=migrate path="database migrate"
func Both(ctx cli.Context) error { return nil }

//fabrik:cli:command path="database Migrate"
func BadSegment(ctx cli.Context) error { return nil }

//fabrik:cli:command name=Migrate
func BadName(ctx cli.Context) error { return nil }

//fabrik:cli:command path="database migrate"
func First(ctx cli.Context) error { return nil }

//fabrik:cli:command path="database migrate"
func Second(ctx cli.Context) error { return nil }
-- want/diags --
error: shared/shared.go:5:1: //fabrik:cli:command takes name= or path=, not both
  help: path= owns every segment including the leaf
error: shared/shared.go:8:37: invalid CLI token "Migrate" in path
  help: segments are lowercase kebab-case: [a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*
error: shared/shared.go:11:27: invalid CLI token "Migrate"
  help: names are lowercase kebab-case: [a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*
error: shared/shared.go:17:1: duplicate command "database migrate"
  help: first declared at $WORK/shared/shared.go:14:1; command paths are unique per parent
